lesson.menu_desc: First conditional for offers: If you order 500 units, we will offer a discount + negotiation frames -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 12 -- Negotiation Basics: Terms and Offers
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Roberto Rezende | Aula 12 -- Negotiation Basics: Terms and Offers

listenings.text: Hi Roberto, this is Amy. I am calling to brief you before your negotiation with the supplier tomorrow. Here is our position. We want a lower price, but we also need flexible terms. If they offer a five percent discount, we will sign a one-year deal. If the volume is high, ask them to meet us halfway on the delivery cost. Remember to protect our margin. Do not accept the first offer. If their terms are not flexible, we will look at another supplier. Stay calm, listen first, and make a counteroffer only after you understand what they want. Good luck, and call me after the meeting.

This is a quick summary of my negotiation with the supplier. I started by listening to their offer. Their first price was too high, so I made a counteroffer. I explained that if we place a bulk order, we will need a better discount. They were flexible on the price, but the delivery terms were not good. So we agreed to meet halfway. If they confirm the new terms by Friday, we will sign the deal next week. Overall, I protected our margin and kept a strong relationship with the supplier. I will send Amy the final terms tonight.

extra_audio.text: First, Roberto listens carefully to the supplier's first offer. Then he explains that the price is too high for a bulk order. After that, he makes a counteroffer with better terms. Next, the supplier and Roberto agree to meet halfway on the delivery cost. Finally, they confirm the new terms and plan to sign the deal next week.
